name: npm_package_scraper
title: npm Package Scraper
description: Search the public npm registry or inspect a package, returning package metadata, maintainers, links, dependencies, publish dates, scores, and optional download counts.

seo.title: npm Package Scraper Tool for Registry Metadata and Downloads
seo.description: Search npm packages or inspect exact package metadata with Better Fetch, including versions, maintainers, dependencies, links, publish dates, search scores, and download counts.
seo.intro: npm Package Scraper gives agents a compact way to research JavaScript packages without building registry glue code. Search the public npm registry by keyword or inspect an exact package such as react, typescript, or @types/node. The tool normalizes package names, descriptions, maintainers, repository and homepage links, licenses, publish dates, dependency summaries, search scores, and optional download counts for dependency audits, ecosystem research, package comparison, and developer-tool workflows.

useCases.title: Dependency evaluation
useCases.description: Inspect a package before an agent recommends it, including its latest version, license, maintainers, repository, dependency summary, and recent download count.

useCases.title: Package discovery
useCases.description: Search npm by keyword and return normalized package rows with publisher, maintainers, keywords, npm links, search scores, and built-in weekly or monthly download signals.

useCases.title: Ecosystem monitoring
useCases.description: Track package freshness, popularity, maintainer changes, and dependency surface area for JavaScript libraries that matter to a product, docs, security, or DevRel team.

faqs.question: Does npm Package Scraper require an npm token?
faqs.answer: No. Version 0.1 uses public npm registry and npm download-count endpoints through Better Fetch. It does not access private packages or account-specific npm data.

faqs.question: Can it inspect scoped packages?
faqs.answer: Yes. Provide a scoped package such as @types/node or an npm package URL. The tool percent-encodes the slash where npm download endpoints require it.

faqs.question: Does it fetch full README text or every historical version?
faqs.answer: Not in this first slice. Version 0.1 summarizes current package metadata and counts versions, but avoids returning large README bodies or every version row.

mode.description: Extraction mode: inspect one package or search npm packages.

package_name.description: Package name or npm package URL. Required in package mode, e.g. react or @types/node.

query.description: Search query text for npm registry search mode.

sort.description: Search weighting for npm registry search mode.

include_downloads.description: When true in package mode, fetch one npm download-count point for the package.

max_results.description: Maximum search records to return in search mode.

source_url.description: Fetched npm registry or downloads URL

total_matches.description: Total npm search matches when reported

publisher_name.description: Publisher username from npm search results

published_at.description: Latest version publish timestamp or search-result date

version_count.description: Number of versions in package metadata

latest_dependencies.description: Comma-separated dependency names from the latest version

latest_dev_dependencies.description: Comma-separated dev dependency names from the latest version

deprecated.description: Deprecation message for the latest version when present

weekly_downloads.description: Weekly downloads when present in npm search results

monthly_downloads.description: Monthly downloads when present in npm search results

downloads.description: Download count for the selected point period

download_period.description: Download period used for the point count

dependents.description: Dependent count from npm search when present
